articles.title: Lakers Confirm Luka Doncic’s Injury Status Ahead of Rockets Game 2
articles.original_title: Los Angeles Lakers Announce Luka Doncic Injury Decision Before Rockets Game 2 - heavy.com
articles.summary: The Los Angeles Lakers announced that Luka Doncic will miss Game 2 of their first‑round series against the Houston Rockets due to a left hamstring strain. The decision was released before tip‑off, confirming his continued absence for the contest.
key_points: Doncic is sidelined with a left hamstring strain.
He will not play in Game 2 versus Houston.
The announcement was made prior to the game’s start.

articles.title: Experts Update Rockets‑Lakers Game 2 Picks After Kevin Durant Injury News
articles.original_title: Experts release updated Rockets vs. Lakers Game 2 predictions following Kevin Durant injury news - OregonLive.com
articles.summary: Analysts revised their predictions for Game 2 of the Rockets‑Lakers series after learning that Kevin Durant suffered a right knee contusion and is listed as questionable. The injury news prompted adjustments to win‑probability models and betting lines.
key_points: Durant’s right knee contusion makes him a question mark for Game 2.
Expert forecasts and betting odds were modified accordingly.
The updates focus on how Durant’s status could affect the Rockets’ chances.

articles.title: Lakers Injury Update: Kevin Durant Questionable; Luka Doncic & Austin Reaves Out for Game 2
articles.original_title: Lakers Injury Update: Kevin Durant Questionable; Luka Doncic & Austin Reaves Out For Game 2
articles.summary: The Lakers reported that Luka Doncic (left hamstring strain) and Austin Reaves (left oblique strain) will miss Game 2 of the first‑round playoff series, while Rockets star Kevin Durant is listed as questionable after a right knee contusion. The report outlines the health status of key players for both teams.
key_points: Doncic and Reaves are ruled out for Game 2.
Durant’s knee contusion leaves his availability uncertain.
Injuries affect the backcourt depth for both clubs.

articles.title: Luka Doncic Boosts Lakers Playoff Run From the Bench

articles.summary: Even while not starting, Luka Doncic has provided a significant spark for the Lakers in the playoffs, contributing valuable minutes and production off the bench. His impact highlights the team’s depth and flexibility in the postseason.
key_points: Doncic delivers strong bench contributions in the playoffs.
His performance adds depth to the Lakers’ rotation.
The bench play helps sustain the team’s competitive edge.

articles.title: Lakers Set to Pick 25th in 2026 NBA Draft
articles.original_title: Lakers To Pick 25th In 2026 NBA Draft
articles.summary: The NBA announced that the Los Angeles Lakers will hold the 25th overall pick in the first round of the 2026 draft after a tie‑breaker coin flip with the New York Knicks, both finishing the season 52‑29. The placement determines the team’s position in the upcoming draft lottery.
key_points: Lakers receive the 25th pick in the 2026 first round.
The slot was decided by a coin flip after a 52‑29 tie with the Knicks.
Draft position will influence the Lakers’ future roster planning.

articles.title: Where Will the Lakers Pick in the 2026 NBA Draft?

articles.summary: Lakers Nation discusses the likely draft position for Los Angeles in the 2026 NBA Draft, weighing the team's recent performance and lottery odds. The piece outlines scenarios that could push the Lakers into the late first round or early second round.
key_points: Lakers' 2025‑26 season record will heavily influence their lottery odds.
Analysts project a likely pick range between the 20th and 30th spots.
Potential trade options could alter the original draft slot.

key_points: James has not confirmed a retirement date, leaving the front office in a planning limbo.

The organization is maintaining flexibility in contract negotiations and salary‑cap management.

articles.title: Lakers Lose Tiebreaker to Knicks, Secure 25th Pick in Draft
articles.original_title: Lakers lose tiebreaker to Knicks, will have 25th pick in NBA draft - AOL.com
articles.summary: AOL.com details how the Lakers fell short in a tiebreaker against the New York Knicks, resulting in the franchise receiving the 25th overall selection in the upcoming NBA Draft. The article explains the tiebreaker criteria and its impact on the Lakers’ draft strategy.
key_points: Both teams finished with identical records; the Knicks won the tiebreaker based on head‑to‑head results.
Los Angeles will pick 25th overall in the 2026 draft.
The slot positions the Lakers to target late‑first‑round prospects or package the pick in a trade.

articles.title: Wembanyama Becomes First Unanimous Defensive Player of the Year
articles.original_title: Wemby captures DPOY as first unanimous winner
articles.summary: Victor Wembanyama, 22, earned the Defensive Player of the Year award, becoming the youngest ever recipient and the first player to receive every vote. His impact on both ends of the court was highlighted throughout the season.
key_points: Wembanyama is the youngest DPOY winner in NBA history.
He received a unanimous vote, a first for the award.
His defensive versatility was a major factor in his team's performance.

articles.title: Finch Blames Lack of Flopping for Murray’s Free‑Throw Disparity
articles.original_title: Frustrated Finch: 'Maybe we gotta start flopping'
articles.summary: Minnesota Timberwolves head coach Chris Finch expressed frustration after Jamal Murray shot 16 free throws in Game 1, suggesting his players receive fewer calls because they don’t exaggerate contact. Finch called for a change in how the Wolves approach drawing fouls.
key_points: Murray attempted 16 free throws in Game 1, highlighting a disparity.
Finch argued that the Wolves' lack of “flopping” leads to fewer foul calls.
He indicated the team may need to adjust its approach to drawing fouls.

articles.title: Mitchell, Harden Lead Cavs to 2‑0 Series Edge Over Raptors
articles.original_title: Mitchell, Harden help Cavs cruise to 2-0 lead
articles.summary: Donovan Mitchell scored 30 points and James Harden added 28 as the Cleveland Cavaliers took a 2-0 lead in their playoff series against the Toronto Raptors. The Cavs never trailed and built a comfortable margin throughout the game.
key_points: Cavaliers won the second straight game, moving up 2-0 in the series.
Mitchell’s 30 points and Harden’s 28 were the offensive catalysts.
Cleveland maintained the lead for the entire contest, never trailing.

articles.title: Green Says Kerr’s Tenure Likely Ended After This Season

key_points: Green hinted this season may be Kerr’s last with the Warriors.

Comments came amid broader discussions about the franchise’s coaching outlook.

articles.title: NBA Breaks Draft Ties; Jazz Secure Protected Pick
articles.original_title: NBA breaks draft ties; Jazz keep protected pick
articles.summary: The league completed its tiebreaking procedures for the 2026 NBA Draft, establishing the order for picks 15 through 30 and resolving two ties among lottery teams. The Utah Jazz retained a protected pick as a result of the process.
key_points: Tiebreaking determined draft positions 15‑30 for the 2026 draft.
Two ties among lottery teams were resolved.
The Utah Jazz kept a protected draft pick.

key_points: No fan T‑shirts will be given away for Games 3 and 4 vs. Spurs.
